name: PTO IDE Frame
description: Upper-level PTO IDE framework shell for standalone pages and VS Code webviews. It owns IDE chrome, a 4:3 host window ratio, a 100%-intensity multi-point gradient IDE skin, 80% filled translucent blurred pane wells, dense pane typography, utility window controls, optional activity rail, pane-local preview tabs, domain toolbars, status strips, preview surfaces, renderer slots, inspector docks, optional bottom terminal panel replacement, optional floating playback mounting, and split initialization. Standalone explorer defaults to a 300px initial width when the host width is known. Top chrome is transparent over the IDE window, does not draw divider borders, and does not include macOS traffic-light controls. Product pages provide all domain content. It delegates resize behavior to workbench-shell and playback chrome to floating-playback-control.

visualContract: patterns/ide-frame/pattern.css owns the default IDE skin: --ide-gradient-bg, --ide-aura-bg, --ide-frame-pane-fill at 80%, and --ide-frame-pane-backdrop-filter
patterns/ide-frame/pattern.js initializes declared split slots through PtoWorkbenchShell
patterns/ide-frame/pattern.js toggles a right-top terminal control through data-ide-toggle="terminal" and swaps bottom terminal/visualization panels
patterns/ide-frame/pattern.js mounts playback slots through PtoFloatingPlaybackControl
patterns/ide-frame/vscode.css maps --vscode-* variables for extension webviews

hosts.contract: May render PTO-owned topbar, window controls, optional activity rail, explorer, pane-local preview tabs, playback, and local status strip. Tabs belong inside the preview/editor pane, not as a separate top-level chrome band.

Must hide standalone chrome and rely on VS Code for explorer, editor tabs, search, git, terminal, settings, command palette, keybindings, global status, notifications, progress, theme colors, and baseline fonts.

allowedOverrides: host mode through data-host="standalone" or data-host="vscode-webview"
solid pane and control compositing through data-surface="solid", which preserves the frame layout while replacing translucent fills, blur, and glass shadows with semantic solid surfaces
hiding the standalone activity rail through hidden, data-activity-rail="hidden", or data-hide-activity-rail="true"
host window width while preserving the default 4:3 frame ratio
initial pane pixel sizing through data-pixel-sizes, with the standalone explorer defaulting to 300px
right-top terminal toggle through data-ide-toggle="terminal" plus a bottom dock with data-ide-bottom-panel="terminal" and optional data-ide-bottom-panel="visualization"

IDE semantic tokens such as --ide-frame-page-bg, --ide-frame-bg, --ide-gradient-bg, --ide-aura-bg, --ide-frame-chrome-bg, --ide-frame-pane-bg, --ide-frame-pane-fill, --ide-frame-pane-header-fill, --ide-frame-pane-backdrop-filter, --ide-frame-pane-shadow, --ide-frame-pane-border, --ide-frame-header-height, --ide-frame-title-font, and --ide-frame-meta-font
pane content, renderer implementations, inspector content, floating playback state, and command bindings
forbiddenOverrides: replacing the shared IDE gradient skin, 80% pane fill, or pane backdrop blur with page-local opaque panels unless the shared pattern variables are updated first
adding standalone topbar or preview tabstrip background fills and divider borders
showing terminal and bottom visualization panels at the same time; they are mutually exclusive views of the same bottom dock
embedding a private design-system copy, local upload assets, remote framework runtimes, transpiler runtimes, or one-off theme files
showing fake VS Code explorer, editor tabs, global status bar, search, git, terminal, settings, notifications, or progress in data-host="vscode-webview" mode

shipping business sample data, file names, kernel names, graph node data, timeline lanes, trace data, inspector values, placeholder tab names, placeholder code rows, or default textual slot content inside ide-frame
recreating playback footer, scrubber, collapse state, or playback shell chrome in ide-frame-local CSS instead of floating-playback-control
editing source code in the webview; use optional read-only slots and route real edits to the VS Code editor
agentReuseRule: Use ide-frame for IDE-like PTO pages as a framework shell only. Load root design-system tokens, workbench-shell, and optional floating-playback-control only when playback is requested; set data-host for standalone or vscode-webview, declare data-ide-split and data-ide-pane slots, add a right-top data-ide-toggle="terminal" button when a bottom terminal is needed, and initialize PtoIdeFrame. Standalone IDE pages must consume the shared IDE skin from pattern.css: the 100%-intensity multi-point gradient background, aura layer, 80% pane fill, 20px cursor-dot grid, and backdrop blur are defaults, not product-local CSS. Keep the shared pattern clean: put all tabs, titles, file trees, code previews, charts, terminal text, inspector copy, and domain placeholders in the consuming page. Do not style workbench-shell internals; it is only the resize kernel. Do not recreate playback chrome; call PtoFloatingPlaybackControl only when playback is explicitly needed.
